title: Instrumentality and Death
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality and Death             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:16 pm Does instrumentality bring people back to life? Or were we simply tricked into thinking people died? In particular, I'm wondering about Misato, Ritsuko, and Asuka. Misato is the one I've been wondering the most about. In 25' I was given the impression that she died after kissing Shinji. However, her body was later shown to be tanged, and she clearly underwent instrumentality in 25. She also appears in various other instrumentality scenes in both the TV show and EoE. Ritsuko seems the least likely to be still alive, since in the instrumentality sequences, we only see her from the perspective of other characters. However, her body was shown to be tanged in 26'. Finally, we never actually see what happens to Asuka herself in 25'. Rather, we only see what happens to Unit-02. However, the damage that it underwent seems pretty destructive and unrecoverable to me. Yet, Asuka not only undergoes instrumentality, (as seen in 25, among other things) but she returns in the final scene of 26'. So what exactly is going on here? If I had to take a guess, it's that Misato and Asuka actually didn't die but simply came very close to death. Ritsuko died, but appeared in instrumentality because other people (Shinji, mainly) were imagining her.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:22 pm Misato died. Her body was literally torn to pieces by the explosion just after Shinji went up the elevator.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:42 pm Sachi wrote: literally torn to pieces by the explosion

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Feb 15, 2011 6:56 pm Instrumentality brings people back to life, and Asuka died.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Tue Feb 15, 2011 7:36 pm But if Misato's body was blown apart, is the LCL here just from other people nearby or something?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Feb 15, 2011 7:37 pm rhfxz1s wrote: But if Misato's body was blown apart, is the LCL here just from other people nearby or something?[/img]

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:23 pm I'm positive there have been threads on this before, but it's really one of those in universe mysteries that's impossible to answer conclusively. I mean, we know that Ritsuko, Misato, and Asuka were definitely dead, yet all appear in instrumentality. If you consider Kaji, who appears in episodes 25 and 26, then we can say that instrumentality saves people from death that have even been dead quite a while. How this would actually work in the world of NGE and souls, I have no idea and I doubt anyone does either.

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Tue Feb 15, 2011 10:47 pm I always assumed that even those who were killed could come back. If we assume Misato can't return simply because she was desenergrated doesn't mean she couldn't rematerialize through the power of Third Impact Deus Ex Machina.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Feb 15, 2011 11:51 pm rhfxz1s wrote: But if Misato's body was blown apart, is the LCL here just from other people nearby or something?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Feb 16, 2011 1:50 pm Probably whoever had a Rei floating above them lives. Asuka wasn't shown with a floating Rei, but she died in the same timeframe. We just weren't shown hers cuz the maker probably thought it would ruin her epic death scene.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Feb 16, 2011 4:39 pm And to clarify something for rhfxz1s, bodies are tanged by Rei after people die because in the Evangelion universe, it seems to be that the soul stays in the corpse after death.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Wed Feb 16, 2011 10:14 pm Interesting. Well thanks for the responses everyone, I guess instrumentality is just one of those things that can never be fully understood.
